title: buglossoides arvensis subsp. splitgerberi
intro: Buglossoides arvensis subsp. splitgerberi, also known as Splitgerber's bugloss or Bugloss, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is a perennial plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, due to its rich content of flavonoids and saponins. The herbal preparations of Bugloss include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in folk medicine to make poultices and salves.
